 {"id":"bd-2m5","title":"SIMD-accelerated cell comparison and buffer operations","description":"# SIMD/autovec accelerated hot paths (NO unsafe)

## Objective
Improve hot-path performance (cell comparisons, clears, copies, ASCII width checks) **without introducing unsafe code**.

Project constraint: `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` across our crates. We must not create an “unsafe-only SIMD crate”.

## Plan Alignment
The plan mentions optional SIMD acceleration. In this repo, that must translate into one of:
- compiler autovectorization via carefully-written scalar loops
- safe portable SIMD (only if available without `unsafe` in our Rust toolchain)
- leveraging well-vetted dependencies that encapsulate unsafe internally (our crate remains `forbid(unsafe_code)`)

## Scope
Target the following operations, in priority order:
1. Cell equality comparisons in diff scan
2. Buffer clear/fill
3. Row/rect copies
4. ASCII-width fast paths

## Strategy
- Write scalar loops that are friendly to autovec:
  - contiguous slices
  - minimal branching
  - row-major iteration
- Use benchmark-driven iteration:
  - measure p50/p95 for 80x24 and larger terminals
  - confirm no regressions in correctness tests

## Explicit Non-Goal
- Adding `unsafe` blocks or `#[target_feature]` functions inside ftui code.

If later we decide explicit unsafe SIMD is required, that needs an explicit policy change (and likely an ADR + safety review).

## Tests Required
- Property tests confirming optimized paths exactly match the reference scalar behavior.
- Benchmarks that show improvements and include regression budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Optimizations preserve correctness (property tests pass; byte-for-byte identical results where applicable).
- [ ] Benchmarks demonstrate measurable improvements on at least one key hot path.
- [ ] No unsafe code is introduced in ftui crates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:20:50.805814276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:51:43.104701371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:51:43.104678769Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2m5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:22:46.216463669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mlp","title":"Implement Frame Caching tests","description":"# Implement Frame Caching tests

## Background
Part of Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation (bd-6qxh). Tests ensure caching works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- cached_widget_reuses_buffer: Second render uses cached buffer
- mark_dirty_triggers_rerender: Dirty flag forces fresh render
- resize_invalidates_cache: Area change triggers rerender
- hash_detects_content_change: Content change detected and rerendered

## Property Tests
- cache_never_returns_stale_content: After mark_dirty, output is fresh
- resize_always_triggers_rerender: Any area change = fresh render

## Integration Tests
- static_header_cached_effectively: Header only rendered once per resize
- dynamic_content_not_cached: Content widgets always fresh
- memory_usage_bounded: Cache eviction works under memory pressure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples
- [ ] Caching provides measurable performance improvement
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:00.851506009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:16:11.692537489Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:16:11.692517101Z","close_reason":"Frame caching tests already present in crates/ftui-widgets/src/cached.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mlp","depends_on_id":"bd-6qxh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:12.402331929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nzx","title":"Implement Composable Animation Primitives tests","description":"# Implement Composable Animation Primitives tests

- budget_aware_animation_degrades: Animations skip frames under pressure

 {"id":"bd-2rs","title":"Implement clipboard integration (OSC 52)","description":"PHASE 7 EXTRAS - CLIPBOARD INTEGRATION

## Background
OSC 52 allows setting the system clipboard from terminal applications. This enables copy/paste functionality without external dependencies.

## Technical Requirements

### OSC 52 Protocol
Sequence: ESC ] 52 ; c ; <base64-data> BEL

c = clipboard selection:
- c = clipboard
- p = primary (X11)
- s = secondary
- 0-7 = cut buffers

### Clipboard API

pub struct Clipboard {
    caps: TerminalCapabilities,
}

impl Clipboard {
    /// Check if OSC 52 is supported
    pub fn is_available(&self) -> bool {
        self.caps.osc52
    }
    
    /// Set clipboard content
    pub fn set(&self, content: &str, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        if !self.is_available() {
            return Err(io::Error::new(
                io::ErrorKind::Unsupported,
                "OSC 52 not available"
            ));
        }
        
        let encoded = base64::encode(content);
        write!(writer, "\x1b]52;c;{}\x07", encoded)?;
        writer.flush()
    }
    
    /// Clear clipboard
    pub fn clear(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        write!(writer, "\x1b]52;c;\x07")?;
        writer.flush()
    }
}

### Terminal Compatibility
OSC 52 support varies:
- ✅ iTerm2, kitty, alacritty, WezTerm, foot
- ⚠️ tmux (needs set-clipboard on)
- ⚠️ screen (needs clipboard support)
- ❌ Many terminal emulators disable by default

### Detection Strategy
1. Check known terminal from TERM/TERM_PROGRAM
2. Check tmux/screen allow-passthrough
3. Attempt set and check for response (risky)
4. Default: assume unsupported

### Size Limits
Most terminals limit OSC 52 payload:
- Common limit: 74994 bytes (base64)
- Split large content or fail gracefully

### Fallback Options
When OSC 52 unavailable:
- Fall back to external clipboard tool (feature: clipboard-fallback)
  - macOS: pbcopy/pbpaste
  - Linux: xclip, xsel, wl-copy
  - Windows: clip.exe
- Or return error for application to handle

## Tests Required
- [ ] OSC 52 sequence generation
- [ ] Base64 encoding correct
- [ ] Size limit handling
- [ ] Clear clipboard works
- [ ] Capability detection
- [ ] Fallback to external tools
- [ ] Error handling when unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC 52 set clipboard
- [ ] Capability detection
- [ ] Size limit enforcement
- [ ] Clear clipboard
- [ ] Optional external fallback
- [ ] Feature gate: clipboard

 {"id":"bd-2ss","title":"Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility","description":"# Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility

## Background
Plan Section ADR-004 and Section 0.6.6 require a "Windows limitations" section that is honest about what works and what doesn't.

## Document Location
`docs/WINDOWS.md` or `docs/compatibility/windows.md`

## Required Content

### 1. Supported Features (v1)
Document what IS expected to work on Windows:
- [ ] Raw mode enter/exit and cleanup on panic
- [ ] Key input handling (basic keys, modifiers)
- [ ] Resize events
- [ ] Basic mouse support (where Crossterm supports it)
- [ ] Color output:
  - 16 colors (always)
  - 256 colors (Windows Terminal, modern ConHost)
  - TrueColor (Windows Terminal)

### 2. Known Limitations (v1)
Be honest about what MAY NOT work or is best-effort:
- [ ] DEC synchronized output (mode 2026): Not widely supported
- [ ] OSC 8 hyperlinks: Windows Terminal only (cmd.exe/ConHost: no)
- [ ] Bracketed paste: Varies by terminal
- [ ] Focus events: May not work in all environments
- [ ] Kitty keyboard protocol: Limited support
- [ ] Scroll-region optimization: Behavior varies
- [ ] Mouse SGR mode: May fall back to legacy encoding

### 3. Terminal Compatibility Matrix (Windows)
| Feature | Windows Terminal | cmd.exe | ConHost | PowerShell |
|---------|------------------|---------|---------|------------|
| TrueColor | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | depends |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ~ |
| Mouse | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | ~ |
| Sync Output | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |

### 4. Configuration Recommendations
- Recommend Windows Terminal for best experience
- Document any required ConPTY settings
- Explain TERM/COLORTERM environment handling on Windows

### 5. Troubleshooting Section
Common Windows issues:
- "Colors don't show": Check terminal emulator
- "Mouse not working": Enable in terminal settings
- "Cleanup not working": Known ConHost limitation
- "Unicode display broken": Font/codepage issues

### 6. Future Improvements
List what may improve in future versions:
- Better ConHost support
- WSL integration notes
- Specific terminal emulator enhancements

## Cross-References
- Link to ADR-004 for decision context
- Link to terminal compatibility matrix (bd-1un)
- Link to capability detection implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WINDOWS.md exists with all sections
- [ ] Honest about limitations (not marketing fluff)
- [ ] Tested on actual Windows systems
- [ ] Includes actionable troubleshooting
- [ ] Linked from main README.md

## Location
